mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2.matched_phenotypes: urinary urgency, impotence, increased circulating prolactin concentration, acanthosis nigricans, hypertrichosis, hirsutism, papilledema, cirrhosis, hepatosplenomegaly, premature birth, difficulty standing, increased body weight, progressive pulmonary function impairment, lower limb muscle weakness, dystonia, abnormal periungual morphology

cafe au lait spots, precocious puberty, polyostotic fibrous dysplasia, polydactyly, endocrine overactivity of various glands including pituitary and thyroid, growth retardation, diarrhea, spleen enlargement, hepatitis, cardiac defects

café-au-lait macules, freckling in axillary or inguinal regions, iris hamartomas (Lisch nodules), neurofibromas and plexiform neurofibromas, scoliosis, optic glioma, short stature, macrocephaly, hypertelorism, long bone deformities, seizures, learning difficulties

hypogonadism, obesity, small hands and feet, almond shaped eyes with narrow bridge of nose, characteristic facies, mild to moderate mental deficiency, strabismus, hypothalamic dysfunction leading to decreased production of ACTH resulting in short stature, recurring respiratory tract infections, constipation

angiokeratomas, corneal opacifications, hearing loss, tinnitus, pain crises, gastrointestinal symptoms such as bloating, diarrhoea, chronic kidney failure, myoclonus, tremors, neuropathy, reduced sweating ability

skeletal dysplasia characterized by kyphoscoliosis, joint stiffness, herniae, short neck, prominent forehead, thickened tongue, dental crowding, delayed tooth eruption, coarse facial features, short stature, heart valve problems, lung complications, breathing difficulties

epilepsy, intellectual disability, benign tumours called tubers that can develop anywhere in the brain and other organs, skin changes such as ash leaf macules and shagreen patches, eye findings such as RPE alterations, retinal astrocytic hamartomas, and chorioretinal lesions, cardiac rhabdomyomata, renal angiomyolipomas

easy bruising, excessive scar formation, joint hypermobility, fragile blood vessels prone to rupturing, intestinal involvement causing malabsorption, diarrhea, mitral valve prolapse, arterial dissection, hemorrhage into organs, spontaneous intracranial bleeding, splenomegaly, liver cysts, varices, rectal prolapse

psychomotor regression, irritability, feeding problems, vision disturbances, hearing loss, seizures, spastic paraplegia, peripheral motor neuron signs, demyelinative disorder affecting the central nervous system, white matter degeneration, elevated levels of globoid cell leukodystrophy marker (GM1 ganglioside) in serum and CSF
